purpose: Holiday / festival / release / shopping noise stress case used to prevent default ranking and planner distortion.
why_this_second: It should only be run after S1_NEUTRAL is stable, so stress-case behavior is compared against a trustworthy baseline.
It is the anti-distortion sample that tests whether attractive but noisy leisure content overwhelms serious sections.
It is the most important planner stress window for filtering reviews, recaps, shopping spam, and weak festival utility.

core: World, India, Business, and Technology must remain disciplined under holiday/festival noise.
Entertainment may rise naturally, but it must not dominate unrelated sections without gold support.
Festival keywords must not inflate weak or stale items into top positions.
Duplicate same-event clusters must not leak into multiple top slots.
Section bleed must be measured explicitly.
anti_patterns: Entertainment or social stories displacing stronger serious-section candidates without gold support.
Holiday explainers, generic shopping noise, or festival chatter surfacing in Business or Technology without strong section relevance.
Stale festival content or weak keyword matches surfacing due to overweighted temporal or keyword boosts.
Same-event duplicate clusters occupying multiple top positions.

core: Movies, festivals, events, shopping, and alerts should surface only when they are actually future-looking and actionable.
Weekly plan should emphasize real planning utility rather than holiday content volume.
Date extraction should be correct or within allowed bucket tolerance.
Planner output should remain useful even when release and sale content volume is high.
anti_patterns: Movie reviews, recaps, interviews, box-office chatter, or gossip included as planner positives.
Generic sale spam taking planner slots from better alerts or real dated events.
Weakly actionable festival explainers crowding weekly plan output.
Past-only items included without still-actionable value.

hard_rejection_conditions: Entertainment/social occupies more than 3 of front-page top 10 unless gold explicitly supports it.
Business or Technology Recall@10 drops more than 0.05 below stable S1 baseline.
Planner false positives increase more than 0.08 against baseline.
Movie reviews, box office, recap, gossip, or interview content appears in movies or weekly_plan gold matches.
Stale festival explainers leak into serious-section top bands.
execution_order: Draft gold labels for each news section with explicit anti-noise exclusions.
Draft gold labels for each Up Ahead category with aggressive contamination filtering.
Run the app logic on the frozen inputs.
Reject any candidate config that passes aggregate score but fails hard stress conditions.
Recommend no more than 5 bounded config changes.
next_after_this: Create S3_LONG_WEEKEND.v2.strict.json after S2 is in place.
Use S3 to validate temporal and planner robustness once both baseline and anti-distortion cases are defined.
